---
title: "GO Term Enrichment"
site: workflowr::wflow_site
output:
  workflowr::wflow_html:
    toc: false
    toc_float: true
    toc_collapsed: false
    theme: default
    css: "theme3.css"
toc_depth: 1
number_sections: true
editor_options:
  chunk_output_type: console
---
## Instructions for Analysis {.tabset}

### GO Term Enrichment

Gene Ontology (GO) Term enrichment is a common analysis done with transcriptome data, as it provides functional annotation of genes of interest that can be identified through methods such as differential gene expression (DEG) or gene co-expression network analyses. The results from these analyses are then used to create a short list of genes, which are then put through the GO term enrichment process.

#### Data Preparation 

##### List of Genes of Interest
You will need to run some sort of analysis (DEG, co-expression pathway, etc.) to identify the genes you want to perform GO term enrichment on. You will want to narrow this down to exclude genes that are
